description: The restless edge where land meets sea, shaped by tides and salt wind into something neither fully solid nor fully water.
biomes: Beach
Warm sand curving along the waterline. Shells crunch underfoot and the tide leaves new treasures with every retreat.
Cliff
A sheer wall of stone dropping straight into crashing surf. The wind is fierce at the top and the view is staggering.
Jagged Rocks
Rough black stone carved into sharp edges by the sea. Spray erupts with every wave and the footing is treacherous.
Rocky Shore
A tumble of smooth boulders and tide pools stretching to the waterline. Crabs scuttle in the crevices and seaweed clings to everything.
Bay
A sheltered curve of calm water protected from the open sea. The shore is gentle here, and the waves arrive tired and small.
Peninsula
A narrow arm of land reaching into the ocean, battered on three sides by wind and waves. The end feels like standing at the edge of the world.
Lagoon
Shallow turquoise water separated from the sea by a thin strip of sand. The surface is calm and the bottom is visible.
Estuary
Where river meets ocean and the water shifts between fresh and salt. The mud is rich and the birds gather in vast flocks.
Delta
A maze of branching waterways spreading through flat, silty land. The channels shift with the seasons and the ground is never dry.
Tidal Pool
Small rocky basins left behind by the retreating sea, each one a tiny world of anemones, urchins, and darting fish.
Sea Stack
A tall column of stone standing alone in the surf, cut off from the mainland by centuries of erosion.
Fjord
A deep, narrow inlet carved by glaciers and flanked by towering cliffs. The water is dark and still and impossibly deep.
